scope_boundary: synthetic adapter records plus one bounded live temp-file effect/rollback replay and one hand-authored capability-envelope classification; no OS-enforced sandbox, deployed adapter, network isolation, secret-handle safety, approval-service quality, target-service rollback, or support promotion
transition_reason: The repository has exact source mappings, invocation and authority schemas, synthetic permission/adversarial/human-oversight/partition fixtures, one bounded temp-file write with exact rollback restoration, and one five-project capability-envelope record with ten rejecting mutations. The new record separates executable/path/network/clock/randomness scopes, process observation from OS enforcement, playback from bounded live reexecution, irreversible-residual disclosure, and ordinary-dispatch authority. These artifacts still do not establish an OS-enforced sandbox, deployed adapter, network isolation, approval-service quality, secret-handle safety, target-service rollback, historical-project behavior, or runtime safety.
required_artifacts: deployed adapter traces across at least one real tool or runtime target
sandbox isolation tests with attempted forbidden effects
approval-service tests covering expiry, revocation, escalation, and denial receipts
secret-handle safety tests that avoid raw secret exposure in logs and artifacts
rollback execution tests for reversible effects and residual capture for irreversible effects

matched simpler baseline reporting denied/unsafe effects, false accepts/rejects, latency, operator burden, rollback cost, and residual discovery; any independent specialist review is postpublication and not a completion gate
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_runtime_adapter_permissions.py && python3 scripts/validate_runtime_adapter_effect_probe.py && python3 scripts/validate_sandboxed_adapter_capability.py && python3 scripts/validate_source_evidence_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: runtime adapter permission fixtures are synthetic
the live effect is a disposable temp-file probe and not a deployed adapter
process observation did not establish OS sandbox enforcement
schemas validate record shape rather than deployed adapter enforcement
deployed adapter traces, OS sandbox isolation tests, approval-service tests, secret-handle tests, and target-service rollback tests are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: claim text interpreted as deployed adapter behavior or sandbox-safety evidence
effect receipt validity claimed without real effect trace
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the bounded record/effect behavior that passed, or after deployed adapter traces bind exact executable and capability scopes to observed OS enforcement, approval behavior, secret-handle safety, target-service rollback, irreversible residuals, and accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove deployed adapter behavior
schemas validate record shape rather than permission enforcement
approval expiry markers in fixtures do not prove an approval service exists
the temp-file rollback proves only exact restoration in that disposable probe
process observation and declared scopes do not prove OS confinement
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent runtime-security, formal-methods, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no observed OS sandbox isolation test
non_claims: does not promote Runtime Adapters above argument
does not prove deployed adapter behavior
does not prove rollback execution or runtime safety
